title: Rufus Workflow Definition
description: JSON Schema for Rufus workflow YAML files. Provides IDE autocomplete and validation for workflow definitions.

workflow_type.description: Unique identifier for this workflow type. Used when starting workflows.

workflow_version.description: Semantic version for this workflow definition (e.g., '1.0.0'). Recommended for production workflows.

initial_state_model.description: Python import path to the Pydantic model defining this workflow's state structure.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this workflow does.

steps.description: Ordered list of steps that make up this workflow.

name.description: Unique identifier for this step within the workflow. Use descriptive PascalCase names.

type.description: Step execution type. Determines how the step is executed.

function.description: Python import path to the step function. Required for STANDARD, COMPENSATABLE, ASYNC, DECISION step types.

compensate_function.description: Python import path to the compensation function. Used for Saga pattern rollback. Only valid for COMPENSATABLE type or when using Saga mode.

input_model.description: Python import path to Pydantic model for validating user input to this step.

automate_next.description: If true, automatically execute the next step after this one completes. If false, workflow pauses until next_step() is called.

dependencies.description: List of step names that must complete before this step can execute. Step names must reference existing steps in this workflow.

routes.description: For DECISION steps only. Defines conditional routing based on state values.

condition.description: Python expression evaluated against workflow state. First matching condition is taken.

target.description: Name of step to jump to if condition is true.

dynamic_injection.description: Rules for injecting new steps at runtime based on workflow state. USE WITH EXTREME CAUTION - makes workflows non-deterministic.

condition.description: Python expression evaluated after this step. If true, injects steps.

steps.description: Steps to inject if condition is true.

insert_after.description: Step name after which to insert the new steps.

tasks.description: For PARALLEL steps only. List of tasks to execute concurrently.

name.description: Unique name for this parallel task.

function.description: Python import path to the task function.

merge_strategy.description: How to merge async/parallel step results into workflow state. SHALLOW = top-level keys only, DEEP = recursive merge.

merge_conflict_behavior.description: How to handle conflicts when parallel tasks return overlapping keys. PREFER_NEW = use task result, PREFER_EXISTING = keep current state, RAISE_ERROR = fail.

allow_partial_success.description: For PARALLEL steps. If true, workflow continues even if some tasks fail. If false, any task failure fails the step.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum execution time for this step in seconds. Step fails if timeout is exceeded.

http_config.description: For HTTP steps only. Configuration for HTTP request.

url.description: HTTP endpoint URL. Can include Jinja2 templates using workflow state.

headers.description: HTTP headers. Values can be Jinja2 templates.

body.description: Request body for POST/PUT/PATCH. Can include Jinja2 templates.

target_workflow_type.description: For FIRE_AND_FORGET and CRON_SCHEDULER steps. Workflow type to launch.

initial_data_template.description: For FIRE_AND_FORGET and CRON_SCHEDULER steps. Template for initial data passed to launched workflow. Can include Jinja2 templates.

cron_expression.description: For CRON_SCHEDULER steps only. Cron expression defining schedule.

schedule_name.description: For CRON_SCHEDULER steps only. Optional name for this schedule.

loop_body.description: For LOOP steps only. Steps to execute on each iteration.

mode.description: For LOOP steps only. ITERATE = loop over list items, WHILE = loop while condition is true.

iterate_over.description: For LOOP steps with ITERATE mode. State path to list to iterate over (e.g., 'items', 'data.users').

item_var_name.description: For LOOP steps with ITERATE mode. Variable name for current item in loop body.

while_condition.description: For LOOP steps with WHILE mode. Python expression evaluated before each iteration.

max_iterations.description: For LOOP steps. Maximum number of iterations to prevent infinite loops.
